NetworkPolicy to allow egress to Linear, Discord, GitHub, Nous APIs and kube-dns

subtasks.title: Create sigma1-dev Kubernetes namespace
Provision sealed secrets for Linear API, Discord webhook, GitHub PAT, and NOUS_API_KEY
Create sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap
Author Helm values file for single-replica dev deployments
Apply network policies for egress to Linear, Discord, GitHub, and Nous APIs
Validate infrastructure with a test pod

decision_points.question: How should agent hints be matched to Linear users — by display name substring, exact match, or via a custom metadata field on Linear user profiles?
decision_points.context: The resolve_agent_delegates function needs to map strings like 'bolt', 'nova', 'blaze' to Linear user IDs. Display name matching is fragile if names change; a custom metadata/label approach is more robust but requires setting up that metadata on each Linear user.
options: Use a custom Linear user attribute or external config map of agent-hint-to-userId

subtasks.title: Implement resolve_agent_delegates() function with Linear Users API query
subtasks.description: Create a new TypeScript module exporting resolve_agent_delegates(agentHints: string[]) that queries the Linear GraphQL users endpoint, matches agent hint strings to Linear user profiles, and returns a Map<string, string> of agentHint → linearUserId.
subtasks.details:
1. Create a new file `src/lib/resolve-agent-delegates.ts`.
2. Define the function signature: `async function resolve_agent_delegates(agentHints: string[]): Promise<Map<string, string>>`.
3. Use the Linear GraphQL API (`users` query) to fetch all workspace users. Filter/match each agent hint to a user by display name (or chosen matching strategy per decision point).
4. For any hint that cannot be resolved, log a structured warning (agent hint, reason) and exclude it from the returned map — do NOT throw.
5. Return the populated map.
6. Export the function as a named export for consumption by the issue creation module.
7. Ensure the Linear API token is read from environment variables sourced via `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap (`envFrom`).
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test with a mocked Linear GraphQL client returning 3 known users. Verify: (a) all 3 known hints resolve correctly, (b) an unknown hint returns no entry and produces a warning log, (c) function returns a Map with expected size.

subtasks.title: Add per-pipeline-run caching layer to resolve_agent_delegates()
subtasks.description: Wrap the Linear Users API call inside resolve_agent_delegates with an in-memory cache scoped to a single pipeline run so repeated calls within the same run reuse the first result.
subtasks.details:
1. Inside `resolve-agent-delegates.ts`, introduce a module-level cache variable (e.g., `let cachedDelegates: Map<string, string> | null = null`).
2. On first invocation, perform the API call and store the result. On subsequent invocations within the same process/run, return the cached result immediately.
3. Export a `clearDelegateCache()` function to allow explicit cache invalidation between pipeline runs or in tests.
4. Add a debug-level log line when serving from cache vs. fetching fresh.
5. Keep the cache simple — no TTL needed since pipeline runs are short-lived.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test: call resolve_agent_delegates twice with the same hints; assert the Linear API mock is called exactly once. Call clearDelegateCache(), then call again; assert the mock is called a second time.

subtasks.title: Integrate resolve_agent_delegates into issueCreate mutation flow
subtasks.description: Modify the existing task-to-issue mapping module to call resolve_agent_delegates() before each issueCreate GraphQL mutation and pass the resolved linearUserId as the assigneeId field in the mutation payload.
subtasks.details:
1. Locate the existing issue creation call site (the `issueCreate` GraphQL mutation in the PM server).
2. Before the loop/call that creates issues, invoke `resolve_agent_delegates()` with the collected array of agent hint strings from the decomposed tasks.
3. For each task/issue being created, look up the task's agent hint in the returned map.
4. If a userId is found, include `assigneeId: userId` in the `issueCreate` mutation variables.
5. If no userId is found (hint was unresolvable), omit `assigneeId` so the issue is created unassigned.
6. Ensure the mutation payload type/interface is updated to accept the optional `assigneeId` field.
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test: with a mocked Linear API, submit a pipeline run with 3 tasks having hints 'nova', 'bolt', 'unknown'. Verify the issueCreate calls for 'nova' and 'bolt' include correct assigneeId values, and the 'unknown' task's call omits assigneeId.

subtasks.title: Remove legacy agent:pending label code
subtasks.description: Find and remove all code paths that set or reference the 'agent:pending' label on Linear issues as a fallback assignment mechanism.
subtasks.details:
1. Search the PM server codebase for all references to 'agent:pending' (string literals, label creation calls, label filtering logic).
2. Remove label creation/attachment in the issueCreate mutation payload and any post-creation label-setting calls.
3. Remove any helper functions or constants that define the 'agent:pending' label ID or name.
4. Check for any webhook handlers or scheduled jobs that react to the 'agent:pending' label and remove/update them.
5. If there are Linear labels already created in the workspace, note in a comment/README that existing 'agent:pending' labels on old issues can be cleaned up manually.
subtasks.test_strategy: Grep the entire codebase for 'agent:pending' — zero results expected. Run existing test suite to confirm no regressions from removed code paths.

subtasks.title: Add structured logging for agent delegation in issue creation
subtasks.description: Add structured log entries at each issue creation that include the issue ID, issue title, agent hint, and resolved delegate_id (or 'unassigned').
subtasks.details:
1. After each successful `issueCreate` call, emit a structured log object: `{ event: 'issue_created', issueId, title, agentHint, delegateId: resolvedId ?? 'unassigned' }`.
2. Use the project's existing structured logger (e.g., pino, consola, or Bun's console with JSON formatting).
3. At the start of agent resolution, log the full list of agent hints being resolved.
4. At the end of the pipeline run's issue creation phase, log a summary: total issues created, count assigned, count unassigned.
5. Ensure log level is 'info' for normal operations and 'warn' for unresolvable hints.
subtasks.test_strategy: Capture log output during a test pipeline run. Verify each created issue produces a structured log entry with all required fields. Verify the summary log shows correct counts.

subtasks.title: Write unit and integration tests for agent delegation flow
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive test coverage for resolve_agent_delegates, the updated issueCreate integration, cache behavior, and end-to-end pipeline delegation.
subtasks.details:
1. **Unit tests for resolve_agent_delegates:**
   a. Mock Linear users API returning 3 agents (bolt, nova, blaze). Assert correct map returned.
   b. Include an unknown hint. Assert it's excluded from map and a warning is logged.
   c. Simulate Linear API failure. Assert graceful degradation (empty map, error logged).
2. **Unit tests for cache:**
   a. Verify second call doesn't hit API. Verify clearDelegateCache resets.
3. **Integration test for issueCreate with assigneeId:**
   a. Mock Linear issueCreate mutation. Submit a task with hint 'nova'. Assert assigneeId is present in mutation variables.
   b. Submit a task with unresolvable hint. Assert assigneeId is absent.
4. **Regression test for legacy label removal:**
   a. Run issue creation flow. Assert no 'agent:pending' label is attached to any created issue.
5. **End-to-end test:**
   a. Run a full pipeline with 5+ tasks. Query created issues and verify non-null assigneeId on all resolvable hints.
6. Use Bun's built-in test runner (`bun test`).
subtasks.test_strategy: All tests pass with `bun test`. Coverage report shows >90% line coverage for resolve-agent-delegates.ts and the modified issue creation module. CI pipeline gate on test pass.

deliberation_module.conditional_check: If NOUS_API_KEY is set, enable Hermes research integration; else, log info and skip.

response_handling.error_handling: On timeout or error, log and return null

research_memo.integration: Append to deliberation output if results exist

artifact_storage.condition: If Hermes was called, write raw response

unit_tests: Hermes client: returns filtered results, handles missing key, handles timeout, handles HTTP/malformed errors, handles empty results
Research memo: formats results, handles empty input, preserves special characters
integration_tests: Deliberation pipeline: with/without NOUS_API_KEY, Hermes section present/absent, artifact file written, directory created

task_breakdown.title: Implement Design Snapshot PR Surfacing (Blaze - React/Next.js)
task_breakdown.description: Build a web frontend component that surfaces design snapshot PRs generated during the pipeline, allowing users to view PR details, design deltas, and navigate to the GitHub PR. This addresses the hasFrontend=true, targets=web requirement from the design intake.

subtasks.title: Create page route and data-fetching hook for design snapshot PRs
details: Create the file app/pipeline/[runId]/design-snapshots/page.tsx (or pages/pipeline/[runId]/design-snapshots.tsx depending on the project's Next.js routing convention).
Extract runId from the route params.
Implement useDesignSnapshotPRs(runId) in a hooks/ directory. Use fetch or the project's existing HTTP client to call GET /api/pipeline/${runId}/prs. Return { data, isLoading, error } shape.
Handle non-200 responses by throwing or returning an error object.

The page component should call this hook and pass state down to child components (built in subsequent subtasks). For now, render a placeholder wrapper <div> that conditionally renders based on isLoading, error, and data.
Use Tailwind CSS for the page layout container.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test the hook with msw or a fetch mock: verify it returns loading=true initially, resolves to data on 200, and returns an error object on 500. Verify the page route renders without crashing when given a valid runId.

subtasks.title: Build DesignSnapshotList component with PR cards, loading skeletons, and error state
details: Create components/DesignSnapshotList.tsx. Accept props: { prs, isLoading, error, onSelectPR }.
Loading state: When isLoading is true, render 3 skeleton cards using Tailwind's animate-pulse on placeholder divs matching card dimensions.
Error state: When error is truthy, render an error banner with the message and a 'Retry' button (calls a retry callback prop).
Empty state: When prs is an empty array, render a centered message: 'No design snapshots found for this run.'

Populated state: Map over prs and render a card for each: PR title as an <a> linking to pr.url (opens in new tab with rel='noopener noreferrer'), PR number displayed as #${pr.number}, Status badge: green for 'open', purple for 'merged', red for 'closed'. Use a <span> with appropriate Tailwind bg/text classes, Files changed: e.g., '5 files changed', Relative timestamp using a lightweight formatter (e.g., Intl.RelativeTimeFormat or a small utility function — no heavy dependency), The entire card is clickable (calls onSelectPR(pr.number)) to trigger the diff viewer.
Cards should use a CSS grid layout: single column on mobile (<640px), 2 columns on tablet, 3 columns on desktop.
subtasks.test_strategy: Component test: render with 3 mocked PRs, assert 3 cards exist with correct titles, status badge colors, and GitHub links. Render with empty array, assert 'No design snapshots' message. Render with isLoading=true, assert skeleton elements. Render with error, assert error message and retry button.

subtasks.title: Implement DesignDeltaViewer component with diff library integration
details: Install react-diff-viewer-continued v3.x: npm install react-diff-viewer-continued.
Create components/DesignDeltaViewer.tsx. Accept props: { runId, prNumber, onClose }.

Implement a hook useDesignDiff(runId, prNumber) that fetches GET /api/pipeline/${runId}/prs/${prNumber}/diff. Expect the response to contain { files: [{ filename, old_content, new_content }] } (or adapt to the actual API shape).
Loading state: Show a skeleton or spinner while the diff is loading.
Error state: Show an error message with a 'Retry' button.

Diff rendering: For each file in the diff response, render a collapsible section with the filename as header. Inside, render <ReactDiffViewer oldValue={old_content} newValue={new_content} splitView={splitView} /> where splitView is toggled by a button.
Add a toggle control at the top: 'Side-by-side' vs 'Inline' view mode.
Add a 'Close' button or back-navigation that calls onClose.
Style the viewer container to be full-width with horizontal scroll for wide diffs on narrow viewports.
Integrate into the page: when onSelectPR is called from DesignSnapshotList, show the DesignDeltaViewer (e.g., as a slide-in panel or replacing the list with a back button).
subtasks.test_strategy: Component test: render with mocked diff API returning 2 files, verify both file sections appear with diff content. Test the side-by-side/inline toggle changes the splitView prop. Test loading state renders spinner/skeleton. Test error state shows error message. Test close button calls onClose.

subtasks.title: Accessibility pass: ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support
details: PR cards (DesignSnapshotList): Each card should be a <button> or have role='button' and tabIndex={0} with onKeyDown handling Enter/Space to trigger selection. Add aria-label to each card: e.g., 'View diff for PR #42: Update design tokens, status open'. Status badges should have aria-label describing the status (not rely on color alone). The GitHub link inside the card must be separately focusable and have aria-label='Open PR #42 on GitHub (opens in new tab)'. Loading skeletons should have aria-busy='true' and aria-label='Loading design snapshots'. Error state retry button should have aria-label='Retry loading design snapshots'.

DesignDeltaViewer: The view mode toggle should be a proper <button> group or radio group with role='radiogroup' and aria-label='Diff view mode'. Collapsible file sections should use <details>/<summary> or aria-expanded on toggle buttons. The diff viewer output should be wrapped in a role='region' with aria-label='Diff for filename.ext'. Close button should have aria-label='Close diff viewer' and focus should return to the triggering card on close.
Focus management: When navigating from list to diff viewer, focus the first element in the diff viewer. When closing, return focus to the previously selected card.
Verify no color-only indicators — all status badges also have text labels.
subtasks.test_strategy: Run axe-core programmatically on the rendered page in each state (loading, populated, empty, error, diff view open). Assert zero critical or serious violations. Tab through all interactive elements and verify logical focus order. Verify focus moves to diff viewer on card activation and returns on close.

subtasks.title: Responsive layout implementation for 320px to 1920px viewports
details: Card grid breakpoints (verify/refine from subtask 4002): 320px–639px: single column, cards full width with appropriate padding (min 16px side padding). 640px–1023px: 2-column grid. 1024px–1920px: 3-column grid. Ensure card content (especially long PR titles) wraps or truncates with ellipsis and a title tooltip.

Diff viewer responsiveness: On viewports <768px, force inline view mode (side-by-side is unusable) and hide the split-view toggle or disable it with a tooltip explaining why. Wrap the diff viewer in overflow-x-auto so wide lines scroll horizontally within the container. File section headers should be sticky on scroll.
Page-level layout: Ensure the page header, breadcrumbs (if any), and content area have appropriate max-width (e.g., max-w-7xl mx-auto) and side padding.
Test that at 320px, no element causes horizontal page scroll (use overflow-x: hidden on body as a safeguard is NOT acceptable — fix the actual overflow).
subtasks.test_strategy: Visual regression: capture screenshots at 320px, 480px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px widths for both the list view and the diff viewer. Verify no horizontal overflow at any width. Verify card grid column count matches spec at each breakpoint. Verify diff viewer forces inline mode below 768px.

subtasks.title: Component and integration tests for all UI states and accessibility audit
details: Use the project's testing framework (likely Vitest + React Testing Library or Jest + RTL).

DesignSnapshotList tests: Render with 3 mocked PRs; assert 3 cards with correct title text, correct href on GitHub links, correct status badge text. Render with empty prs array; assert 'No design snapshots' message. Render with isLoading=true; assert skeleton elements with aria-busy. Render with error; assert error message text and retry button. Click retry; assert callback called. Click a card; assert onSelectPR called with correct PR number. Keyboard: press Enter on focused card; assert onSelectPR called.

DesignDeltaViewer tests: Mock diff API to return 2 files. Render; assert both file sections with filenames. Assert diff content visible. Toggle inline/split view; assert the mode changes. Click close; assert onClose called. Mock diff API to return 500; assert error message.

Accessibility audit tests: For each major state (list loaded, list empty, list error, diff viewer open), render and run axe(container). Assert results.violations has length 0 for critical and serious.
Integration test: Render the full page with both API calls mocked. Click a card, verify diff viewer opens. Close it, verify list reappears and focus returns.
All async assertions should use waitFor or findBy queries.
subtasks.test_strategy: All tests must pass in CI. Verify coverage: DesignSnapshotList (loading, populated, empty, error, click, keyboard), DesignDeltaViewer (loaded, error, toggle, close), axe-core audit per state. Integration test covers the full user flow from list to diff and back.

decision_points: Diff library choice: react-diff-viewer-continued v3.x is suggested, but alternatives like diff2html or a custom implementation could be considered — confirm the library meets bundle-size and accessibility requirements before proceeding.

Design system alignment: stitch_status=failed means building from scratch — determine whether to adopt an existing project design system (if one exists) or default to raw Tailwind CSS utility classes for all components.

plan.title: Implement Discord Notification for Pipeline Start/Complete (Nova - Bun/Elysia)

subtasks.title: Implement DiscordNotifier service class with embed builders and retry logic

steps.details: Create a new file for the DiscordNotifier service class.

steps.step: Read DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL from process.env in constructor
steps.details: Store the webhook URL as a private field. If not set, store null.

details: If webhookUrl is null, log a warning and return.
POST to the webhook URL with Content-Type: application/json and the serialized payload.
On 429 or 5xx response, retry up to 2 times with 1-second delay (await Bun.sleep(1000)).
On final failure after retries, log the error but do NOT throw.

steps.details: Build a Discord embed with color 0x3498db (blue), title '🚀 Pipeline Started', fields for Run ID, PRD Title, Started At. Call sendWebhook.

steps.step: Implement notifyPipelineComplete(runId, prdTitle, status, taskCount, issueCount, prUrl, timestamp)
steps.details: Build a Discord embed with color 0x2ecc71 (green) for success or 0xe74c3c (red) for failure, title '✅ Pipeline Complete' or '❌ Pipeline Failed', fields for Run ID, PRD Title, Status, Tasks Generated, Issues Created, PR URL, Completed At. Call sendWebhook.

steps.step: Export the class as a singleton or for DI
steps.details: Export the DiscordNotifier class for use in other modules.

test_strategy: Unit test: mock global fetch. Call notifyPipelineStart and assert the POST body contains the correct embed structure with blue color, correct title, and all three fields.
Call notifyPipelineComplete with status='success' and verify green color and success title; call with status='failure' and verify red color and failure title with all seven fields present.
Test missing DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: instantiate with env unset, call both methods, verify zero fetch calls and a warning was logged.
Test retry: mock fetch to return 429 on first call then 200 on second; verify two fetch calls were made with ~1s delay.

subtasks.title: Hook DiscordNotifier into the intake pipeline handler

steps.step: Import DiscordNotifier in the pipeline handler
steps.details: Import the DiscordNotifier service in the intake pipeline handler file.

steps.step: Call notifyPipelineStart at the beginning of the handler
steps.details: After extracting runId, prdTitle, and generating a timestamp, call await discordNotifier.notifyPipelineStart(runId, prdTitle, new Date().toISOString()).

steps.step: Wrap main pipeline logic in try/finally
steps.details: Ensure notifyPipelineComplete is called in the finally block, regardless of success or failure.

In the finally block, determine status ('success' or 'failure'), and call await discordNotifier.notifyPipelineComplete(runId, prdTitle, status, taskCount, issueCount, prUrl, new Date().toISOString()).

steps.step: Default values for taskCount, issueCount, prUrl on failure
steps.details: If the pipeline failed before generating these, default to 0 or empty string as appropriate.

steps.step: Ensure notifier errors do not mask pipeline errors
steps.details: The notifier already swallows its own errors, but verify this at the call site.

test_strategy: Integration test: trigger a full pipeline run with a mock/test PRD. Verify that DiscordNotifier.notifyPipelineStart was called once before any task generation begins and DiscordNotifier.notifyPipelineComplete was called once after the pipeline finishes.
Test failure path: inject a deliberate error mid-pipeline and verify notifyPipelineComplete is still called with status='failure' and the pipeline error propagates correctly to the caller.
End-to-end: with a real DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL pointed at a test channel, trigger a pipeline run and confirm two embeds appear (start and complete) with the correct run ID.

acceptance_criteria: DiscordNotifier service class is implemented with correct embed payloads and retry logic.
Pipeline handler calls notifyPipelineStart and notifyPipelineComplete at the correct times.
Notifications are skipped (with warning) if DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL is not set.
Retries are attempted on 429/5xx responses, up to 2 times with 1s backoff.
Unit and integration tests cover all described scenarios.
No notifier error can mask or interfere with pipeline error handling.

task_6.title: Implement Linear-Discord Bridge for Issue Notifications (Nova - Bun/Elysia)

env_vars.name: ENABLE_ISSUE_DISCORD_BRIDGE
env_vars.description: Toggle to enable/disable the Linear-Discord bridge without code changes

subtasks.title: Emit issue.created event from the issue creation flow
subtasks.description: Extend the PM server's Linear issue creation logic to emit an internal `issue.created` event after each successful issue creation, carrying the full notification payload.

implementation.emission_point: After successful Linear API call, non-blocking

subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test: mock Linear API, call issue creation, verify event emission with correct payload after API call.

subtasks.title: Implement LinearDiscordBridge service with 2-second batching and fire-and-forget execution
subtasks.description: Create the `LinearDiscordBridge` service that listens for `issue.created` events, batches events within a 2-second window, formats a purple Discord embed, and sends via the existing DiscordNotifier in a fire-and-forget manner with error logging. Include the ENABLE_ISSUE_DISCORD_BRIDGE configuration toggle.

implementation.error_handling: Log error with issue IDs, do not propagate

subtasks.test_strategy: Unit tests for single, batched, split, disabled, unassigned, Discord failure, and non-blocking cases.

subtasks.title: Write comprehensive unit and integration tests for the Linear-Discord bridge
subtasks.description: Create a full test suite covering single event notification, batched notifications, disabled toggle, Discord failure resilience, and end-to-end integration with the pipeline.

test_cases: Integration test with pipeline and mock Discord endpoint

subtasks.test_strategy: All tests pass with `bun test`. Coverage ≥90%. Integration test verifies end-to-end flow.

test_strategy: Unit test: emit issue.created event; verify DiscordNotifier receives a POST with purple embed containing correct issue title and assignee.
Unit test: emit 5 events within 1 second; verify only 1 batched Discord message is sent containing all 5 issues.
Unit test: set ENABLE_ISSUE_DISCORD_BRIDGE=false; verify no Discord call made.
Unit test: simulate Discord API failure; verify issue creation flow is not blocked and error is logged.
Integration test: run pipeline creating 5+ issues; verify Discord channel receives batched issue notification(s) with correct assignee names.

summary.title: End-to-End Pipeline Integration Test (Tess - Test frameworks)
summary.description: Develop and execute a comprehensive E2E integration test that validates the full Sigma-1 pipeline from PRD intake through deliberation, task generation, issue creation with agent delegation, research memo inclusion, PR surfacing, and Discord/Linear notifications.
summary.test_strategy: 1. All 6 test cases pass in CI with real infrastructure. 2. Test Case 1: pipeline completes within 5-minute timeout with status 'completed'. 3. Test Case 2: >= 5 tasks with non-empty agent fields and >= 3 distinct agents. 4. Test Case 3: >= 5 Linear issues each with non-null assigneeId verified against Linear API. 5. Test Case 4: Hermes section present in research memo when NOUS_API_KEY is configured. 6. Test Case 5: PR exists in 5dlabs/sigma-1 with >= 5 scaffold files. 7. Test Case 6: Discord webhook collector received >= 2 messages with correct content. 8. Suite completes in under 10 minutes total.

decision_points.topic: Test framework choice: bun:test vs vitest
decision_points.context: bun:test is native and zero-config but has fewer matchers and less ecosystem support. vitest offers richer assertion libraries, better watch mode, and broader community plugins. The choice affects how helpers, mocks, and reporters are structured.
decision_points.recommendation: vitest — richer assertion API, better timeout/retry support needed for long-running E2E polling, and compatible with Bun runtime.

decision_points.context: Test Case 6 requires verifying Discord messages were sent. Options include: (a) a lightweight HTTP webhook collector service deployed alongside tests, (b) querying Discord API audit logs or channel message history with a bot token, (c) intercepting outbound webhook calls at the application level via a test double. Each has different credential and infrastructure requirements.
options: Discord Bot API channel history query
decision_points.recommendation: Standalone webhook collector — decouples test from Discord API rate limits and bot token management, and can be a simple Bun HTTP server that logs incoming POST payloads.

subtasks.title: Set up E2E test framework and project scaffolding
Configure CI credential injection from sigma1-dev namespace secrets
Implement Discord webhook collector service for notification verification
Implement Test Case 1: Full Pipeline Completion
Implement Test Case 2: Task Generation with Agent Assignments
Implement Test Case 3: Linear Issue Delegation Verification with Cross-API Validation
Implement Test Case 4: Hermes Research Integration with Conditional Assertions
Implement Test Case 5: PR Creation Verification via GitHub API
Implement Test Case 6: Discord Notification Assertions
Implement test suite orchestration: lifecycle hooks, timeout handling, cleanup, and reporting

test_cases.title: Full Pipeline Completion
steps: Submit a sample PRD to the PM server intake endpoint.
Poll the pipeline status endpoint until status is 'completed' or timeout after 5 minutes.
Assert: pipeline status is 'completed', no fatal errors in logs.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Pipeline status reaches 'completed' within 5 minutes and no fatal errors are present.

test_cases.title: Task Generation with Agent Assignments
steps: Fetch tasks from GET /api/pipeline/:runId/tasks.
Assert: at least 5 tasks returned.
Assert: each task has a non-empty agent field.
Assert: at least 3 distinct agent types are present.
test_cases.pass_criteria: At least 5 tasks, each with a non-empty agent field, and at least 3 distinct agent types.

test_cases.title: Linear Issues with Delegate IDs
steps: Fetch issues from GET /api/pipeline/:runId/issues.
Assert: issue count >= 5.
Assert: each issue has a non-null assigneeId (delegate_id).
Cross-verify with Linear API: query each issue by ID and confirm assignee matches.
test_cases.pass_criteria: At least 5 issues, each with a non-null assigneeId, and assignee matches Linear API.

test_cases.title: Hermes Research Integration
steps: Fetch deliberation artifacts from GET /api/pipeline/:runId/deliberation.
If NOUS_API_KEY is set: assert research memo contains 'Hermes Research Findings' section with at least one entry.
If NOUS_API_KEY is not set: assert research memo does not contain Hermes section and no errors.
test_cases.pass_criteria: With NOUS_API_KEY: Hermes section present with at least one entry. Without: section absent and no errors.

test_cases.title: PR Creation
steps: Fetch PR metadata from GET /api/pipeline/:runId/pr.
Assert: PR URL is non-null and points to 5dlabs/sigma-1.
Assert: PR status is 'open'.
Verify PR contains at least 5 task scaffold files via GitHub API.
test_cases.pass_criteria: PR exists in 5dlabs/sigma-1, is open, and contains at least 5 scaffold files.

test_cases.title: Discord Notifications
steps: Query a Discord audit log or use a test webhook collector.
Assert: at least 2 messages received (pipeline start + complete).
Assert: start message contains run ID; complete message contains task count.
test_cases.pass_criteria: At least 2 Discord messages received, with correct run ID and task count.

files.description: Helm values for production deployment: PM server and frontend replica/resource config, autoscaling, probe, affinity, and ingress values.
Helm template for PM server Deployment, with resource requests/limits, readiness/liveness probes, and anti-affinity.
Helm template for frontend Deployment, with resource requests/limits and readiness/liveness probes.
Helm template for HorizontalPodAutoscaler for PM server.
Helm template for Ingress for PM server API, with TLS and rate limiting.
Helm template for Ingress for frontend, with TLS and CDN cache headers.
Helm template for PodDisruptionBudget for PM server.
Helm template for PodDisruptionBudget for frontend.

decision_points.question: Which ingress controller is deployed in the cluster (nginx-ingress, Traefik, AWS ALB Ingress Controller, etc.)? Rate-limiting annotations and CDN cache-header injection differ significantly between controllers.
options: AWS ALB Ingress Controller with WAF-based rate limiting
decision_points.impact: Determines the exact annotation keys and values used in both Ingress manifests, and whether rate limiting is handled at the ingress layer or via an external WAF.

decision_points.question: Is a Let's Encrypt ClusterIssuer already provisioned in the cluster, and should we use the staging or production ACME endpoint for the initial rollout?
options: Create a new ClusterIssuer using Let's Encrypt production endpoint
Start with Let's Encrypt staging, switch to production after validation
decision_points.impact: Affects whether a ClusterIssuer manifest must be added, and whether TLS certs will be trusted by browsers on first deploy.

subtasks.title: Update Helm production values for PM server replicas and resource limits
Update Helm production values for frontend replicas and resource limits
Configure HorizontalPodAutoscaler for PM server
Create Ingress resource for PM server API with TLS and rate limiting
Create Ingress resource for web frontend with TLS and CDN cache headers
Configure PodDisruptionBudgets for PM server and frontend
Add readiness and liveness probes to PM server deployment
Add readiness and liveness probes to frontend deployment
Configure pod anti-affinity rules for PM server cross-zone distribution
Validate full production hardening deployment end-to-end

test_strategy: Run `helm template . -f values-sigma1-prod.yaml` and confirm Deployments, HPAs, PDBs, and Ingresses render as expected.
Deploy to sigma1-prod namespace and verify:
- HPA: `kubectl get hpa -n sigma1-prod` shows correct min/max/target.
- Ingress: `kubectl get ingress -n sigma1-prod` shows both ingresses with TLS.
- TLS: `curl -I https://api.sigma1.5dlabs.io/health` returns 200 with valid cert.
- CDN: `curl -I https://sigma1.5dlabs.io/_next/static/somefile.js` returns Cache-Control header.
- PDB: `kubectl get pdb -n sigma1-prod` shows correct minAvailable.
- Zero-downtime: delete one PM server pod, confirm no failed requests.
- Anti-affinity: `kubectl get pods -o wide -n sigma1-prod -l app=pm-server` shows pods on multiple nodes/zones.
- Probes: `kubectl describe pod` for both services shows readiness/liveness probes.

namespace_alignment: Existing scaffolds are for sigma1-dev; this task targets sigma1-prod. All new manifests must use sigma1-prod namespace.
secret_management: Both environments use the same set of secrets (linear-api-token, github-pat, discord-webhook-url, nous-api-key).
Production requires automated rotation and RBAC restrictions.
RBAC_and_ServiceAccounts: No RBAC or ServiceAccount scaffolds exist for sigma1-dev; all RBAC, ServiceAccount, and RoleBinding resources must be created for sigma1-prod.
Ensure all Deployments in sigma1-prod use dedicated ServiceAccounts (never default).
network_policy: sigma1-dev has default-deny and allow-external-apis NetworkPolicies.
Production requires stricter policies: frontend can only reach PM server on port 3000; PM server can reach external APIs only; cross-namespace traffic must be blocked.
audit_logging: No audit logging scaffolds exist; must create audit policy and log storage/forwarding for sigma1-prod.
documentation: No security documentation exists; must create docs/security.md with detailed tables and runbook.

file_structure.description: ServiceAccounts for PM server, frontend, and secret rotator in sigma1-prod
Least-privilege Roles for PM server, frontend, and secret rotator in sigma1-prod
RoleBindings for ServiceAccounts to their Roles in sigma1-prod
CronJob to rotate linear-api-token secret every 90 days and restart PM server
CronJob to rotate github-pat secret every 90 days and restart PM server
CronJob to rotate discord-webhook-url secret every 180 days and restart PM server
CronJob to rotate nous-api-key secret every 90 days and restart PM server
Kubernetes audit policy for sigma1-prod: logs all mutating ops and auth failures
PersistentVolumeClaim for audit log storage (if not using external aggregator)
Default deny all ingress/egress in sigma1-prod
NetworkPolicy: frontend pods can only egress to PM server on port 3000
NetworkPolicy: PM server can egress to external APIs and DNS only; ingress from frontend
Security documentation: RBAC, ServiceAccounts, secret rotation, NetworkPolicy, audit logging, incident response

test_stubs:
# RBAC
- kubectl get sa -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl describe role pm-server-role -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl describe role frontend-role -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl describe rolebinding pm-server-rolebinding -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl describe rolebinding frontend-rolebinding -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl get pods -n sigma1-prod -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.serviceAccountName}'

# Secret Rotation
- kubectl get cronjobs -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl create job --from=cronjob/rotate-linear-api-token test-rotation -n sigma1-prod
- kubectl get secret linear-api-token -n sigma1-prod -o jsonpath='{.data}'
- kubectl rollout status deployment/pm-server -n sigma1-prod

# Audit Logging
- kubectl create configmap test-audit --from-literal=key=value -n sigma1-prod
- Check audit log file or aggregator for create event in sigma1-prod
- kubectl delete configmap test-audit -n sigma1-prod

# NetworkPolicy
- kubectl get networkpolicies -n sigma1-prod
- From frontend pod: curl pm-server:3000/health (should succeed)
- From frontend pod: curl https://api.github.com (should fail)
- From pm-server pod: curl https://api.github.com (should succeed)
- From pm-server pod: curl service in another namespace (should fail)

# Documentation
- Confirm docs/security.md exists and contains all required sections

options: External Secrets Operator (requires external secret store, more secure, more infra dependencies)
Kubernetes CronJob (simpler, secrets generated/stored in cluster, less secure)
decision_points.recommendation: Use CronJob for initial implementation; document migration path to External Secrets Operator for future.

options: External log aggregator (Elasticsearch, Loki, CloudWatch, etc.; more scalable, more infra dependencies)
decision_points.recommendation: Default to PVC for now; document how to forward to external aggregator if/when available.
